message:
chore(fmt+clippy): restore workspace gates per frankenjax-mmk4

Apply rustfmt across 23 fj-conformance oracle test files and
fix two manual_contains lint hits in fj-trace so cargo fmt --check
and cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings both
go green again. Tracked as bead frankenjax-mmk4 (set to
in_progress in this commit).

fj-trace
--------
Two clippy::manual_contains hits in SimpleTraceContext shape
inference (crates/fj-trace/src/lib.rs):

  -                  if dims.iter().any(|&d| d == 0) {
  +                  if dims.contains(&0) {
                         return Err(... "window_dimensions must be positive" ...)
                     }

  -                  if st.iter().any(|&s| s == 0) {
  +                  if st.contains(&0) {
                         return Err(... "window_strides must be positive" ...)
                     }

Clippy lints `manual_contains` whenever an `iter().any(|&x| x == y)`
pattern can be expressed as `slice::contains(&y)`. The replacement
is exactly equivalent for `&[u32]` here (we're scanning for a
literal 0). Same behavior, shorter source, lint clean.

Plus a couple of inline `Shape { dims: vec![1, 5, 1] }` test
fixtures got rustfmt-broken across multiple lines because they're
inside larger `vec![...]` trees that already exceed the width
budget; fmt's nested-struct rule then forces the inner Shape onto
multiple lines for consistency.

fj-conformance/tests/*.rs (23 files)
------------------------------------
Pure rustfmt cleanup across the per-primitive oracle suites:

  bitcast_oracle, bitwise_oracle, broadcasted_iota_oracle,
  complex_ops_oracle, complex_oracle, control_flow_oracle,
  conv_oracle, copy_oracle, erf_inv_oracle, expand_dims_oracle,
  gather_scatter_oracle, is_finite_oracle, nextafter_oracle,
  reciprocal_oracle, reduce_precision_oracle, reduce_prod_oracle,
  reduce_window_oracle, rem_oracle, rsqrt_oracle, select_oracle,
  shift_oracle, sign_oracle, split_oracle

Two consistent shapes of edit:

1. `let result = eval_primitive(P, &[input], &params(...)).unwrap();`
   re-wrapped onto multiple lines wherever it pushed past
   max_width. Same with inline `Vec<Literal>` initializer chains
   like `data.into_iter().map(|x| Literal::F32Bits(x.to_bits()))
   .collect()`.

2. Module-level `#![allow(dead_code, clippy::approx_constant)]`
   added to every oracle file. Two reasons:
   - `dead_code`: the oracle suites build a lot of helper
     constructors (make_f32_tensor, make_complex_scalar, etc.)
     that only some test functions inside the file consume; once
     a test gets cfg'd out under a feature gate the helper goes
     "unused" from the compiler's POV.
   - `clippy::approx_constant`: oracle data deliberately contains
     literal 3.14159 / 2.71828 etc. for differential-test inputs
     against scipy / JAX, and we don't want to swap them for
     `std::f64::consts::PI` (the literal IS the input being
     fingerprinted into the oracle).

No test logic changed in any file — every test still asserts the
same shape, same values, same dtype it asserted before. fmt-only
refactor; fingerprints over the oracle suites should stay stable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
